expectations.text: Uses t() for all user-facing strings
expectations.evidence: The t() function for translations is widely known in Vue/Nextcloud apps. Naive Claude with Vue knowledge would typically use a translation function. However, it might use vue-i18n's t() rather than the @nextcloud/l10n t() which has a different signature.

The Nextcloud l10n t() signature requires the app name as the first argument: t('appname', 'key'). Naive Claude might use t('key') (single argument) or $t('key') (Vuex/vue-i18n style), which are wrong for Nextcloud. The Nextcloud-specific signature is not general knowledge.
Defaulting to English is a general software convention. Naive Claude would write English strings by default.

expectations.text: Follows NL Design System CSS variables
expectations.evidence: NL Design System CSS variables (Dutch municipal theming tokens) are Conduction/Dutch-gov-specific. Naive Claude would not know to use var(--color-*) NL Design System tokens — it might use @nextcloud/vue CSS variables but not the NL Design System layer.

notes: Using t() and English defaults are general conventions. The Nextcloud-specific t() signature (app name first) and NL Design System CSS variables are Conduction/Dutch-gov-specific.
